element_introduction_order.mastery_context: First elemental sword; basic slashing and charge attacks before element-specific traversal
Core progression mechanic; defeating enemies grants stat increases (health, defense, attack)
Certain enemies immune to specific elements; requires switching swords for tactical advantage
Second elemental sword; enables ice/frozen passage traversal; effective against ice enemies
Third elemental sword; creates ice bridges for river crossing; puzzle-solving traversal
Fourth elemental sword; final sword before Crystalis; strongest element for endgame enemies

element_introduction_order.first_appears: Distributed throughout game via sage encounters
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Utility spells learned from four sages (Zebu, Tornel, Asina, Kensu); enable long-distance travel and information gathering
Defensive equipment scaling with progression; synergizes with level-based stat increases
Kelbesque, Sabera, Mado, Karmine; each encountered twice with distinct elemental weaknesses; require level gates
Combination of all four elemental swords; obtained before Emperor Draygon confrontation
Reveals dragon-like transformation after defeating false form; ultimate antagonist confrontation

typical_tension_triggers: Entering new dungeon area with unknown layout
Encountering boss with unknown elemental weakness
Traversal puzzles requiring correct sword element
Level-gated enemies immune to current equipment
Long corridors with sustained enemy pressure
Platforming sections (ice climbing, rabbit boots navigation)
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Cyclic tension with exploration discovery (safe towns alternate with dangerous dungeons); progression gated by elemental sword acquisition and leveling; boss encounters provide climactic tension releases

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Shops and NPC areas in towns provide optional equipment and spells; sages accessible via telepathy for optional advice
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Hidden passages exploitable through frame-perfect menu usage and NPC interaction (documented in TAS resources); gaps in hitboxes allow sequence-breaking traversal

difficulty_curve.early_game: Gentle introduction with basic Wind sword combat; early enemy encounters teach elemental weakness system without harsh punishment; towns provide safe progression hubs
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Escalation through elemental sword acquisition (Fire, Water); introduction of Dragonia's Finest Four bosses with level gates; dungeon complexity increases; mid-level enemies require tactical element switching
difficulty_curve.late_game: Steep difficulty spike with Thunder sword acquisition and late-game bosses; long dungeon sequences (Goa fortress); level-60+ enemy gates; dual-phase Draygon encounter (false form then dragon form)
notable_spikes: First encounter with Dragonia's Finest Four (initial element mismatch)
Mt. Sabre North progression without proper level threshold
Evil Island sequence with sustained enemy pressure and platforming (moonjumping/rabbit boots required)
Goa Fortress with paralyzed skeleton enemies and Karmine fight
Emperor Draygon dual-phase final boss battle

notable_level_patterns.description: Dungeons and barriers require specific elemental swords to progress; Fire sword melts ice obstacles, Water sword creates bridges, Wind sword breaks certain walls, Thunder sword activates electrical mechanisms. Forces backtracking or sequence planning
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Mt. Sabre area with ice climbing (Fire sword required), river crossings (Water sword required)

notable_level_patterns.description: Four sages (Zebu, Tornel, Asina, Kensu) distributed across map as information checkpoints and spell-teaching nodes; accessible via telepathy spell for guidance; each associated with one elemental sword
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Distributed: Zebu in Swamp, Tornel in Mt. Sabre South, Asina in Island, Kensu in Goa

notable_level_patterns.description: Towns (Portoa, Leaf, Goa, etc.) function as tension release points; contain shops, NPCs, healing opportunities, and plot triggers; separated from dungeon danger zones
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Portoa (initial hub), Leaf (village rescue plot), Goa (final town before Goa Fortress)

notable_level_patterns.description: Extended dungeon sequences with sustained enemy pressure leading to named boss encounters; Dragonia's Finest Four each encountered in thematic lairs with elemental-specific weaknesses
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Goa Fortress (Karmine's lair with skeleton enemies requiring paralysis strategy), final tower (Draygon's lair)

notable_level_patterns.description: Ice climbing sections, water traversal, rabbit boots navigation, and moonjumping sections require specific equipment and timing; documented TAS tricks exploit frame-perfect movement alternation
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Waterfall area (ice climbing, dolphin ride, Evil Island skip potential), Mt. Sabre slopes

notable_level_patterns.description: Enemies and bosses become hittable only at certain player levels; even with correct elemental sword, insufficient level prevents damage; forces grinding or optimal routing
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Late-game bosses require level 50+; Tower enemies require level 60+
